name: Acueducto, Alcázar y Palacio de Riofrío — circular desde Segovia
region_intro: Touring cycling around Segovia offers a diverse landscape, ranging from tranquil river valleys to challenging mountain passes within the Sierra de Guadarrama. The region is characterized by dramatic limestone gorges carved by rivers like the Duratón, expansive Castilian plains, and varied forests including oak, poplar, and pine. Cyclists can explore routes that traverse these natural features, providing a mix of terrains and elevations. Best touring cycling routes around Segovia The most popular touring cycling route is Eresma Valley Greenway, a 30.6 miles (49.3 km) trail that takes about 4 hours 9 minutes to complete. This moderate route traverses diverse landscapes, including oak groves, poplar groves, and pine forests along the Eresma River. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Acueducto, Alcázar y Palacio de Riofrío — circular desde Segovia, a moderate 20.2 miles (32.5 km) path. This route offers views of historic sites and the surrounding countryside from Segovia. Local touring cyclists also love the Real Sitio de San Ildefonso — circular desde el Embalse del Pontón Alto, a 6.6 miles (10.5 km) trail leading through the area around the Royal Palace of San Ildefonso, often completed in about 44 minutes. Touring cycling around Segovia is defined by river valleys, mountain ranges like the Sierra de Guadarrama, and the open Castilian plains. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Segovia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 2,500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Segovia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Segovia?
A: There are over 250 touring cycling routes available in the Segovia region, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored these routes extensively, with over 2,500 touring cyclists using them.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling in Segovia?
A: Segovia offers a diverse landscape for touring cyclists. You can expect routes traversing tranquil river valleys like the Eresma, dramatic limestone gorges such as the Duratón Canyon, challenging mountain passes within the Sierra de Guadarrama, and expansive Castilian plains with fields of sunflowers and wheat. The terrain includes varied forests of oak, poplar, and pine.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Segovia?
A: Yes, Segovia offers several easy touring cycling routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1107603555/real-sitio-de-san-ildefonso-circular-desde-el-embalse-del-ponton-alto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Real Sitio de San Ildefonso — circular desde el Embalse del Pontón Alto&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.6-mile (10.5 km) trail that takes about 44 minutes to complete, leading through the area around the Royal Palace of San Ildefonso.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Segovia?
A: The touring cycling routes in Segovia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 90 reviews. Cyclists often praise the region's diverse landscapes, from river valleys to mountain passes, and the opportunity to explore historic sites and natural beauty.

Q: Are there any routes that feature natural landmarks like gorges or waterfalls?
A: Yes, the region is home to impressive natural features. The Duratón Canyon Natural Park offers dramatic limestone gorges. You can also find routes near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2714437" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boca del Asno Gorge and Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, which is a pleasant recreational area along the Eresma River, ideal for leisure and enjoying small cascades and pools.

Q: Which touring cycling routes offer views of historic sites or cultural attractions?
A: Many routes integrate Segovia's rich history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1107459203/acueducto-alcazar-y-palacio-de-riofrio-circular-desde-segovia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acueducto, Alcázar y Palacio de Riofrío — circular desde Segovia&lt;/a&gt; route offers views of iconic landmarks like the Roman Aqueduct and the Alcázar. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1107603555/real-sitio-de-san-ildefonso-circular-desde-el-embalse-del-ponton-alto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Real Sitio de San Ildefonso — circular desde el Embalse del Pontón Alto&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the area around the Royal Palace of San Ildefonso with its ornate gardens.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by lakes or reservoirs?
A: Absolutely. Several touring cycling routes in Segovia pass by beautiful lakes and reservoirs. Notable spots include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6313358" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Alta Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5780689" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pontón Alto Dam&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2616012" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Espinar Reservoir (Vado de las Cabras)&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5001356" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pirón Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. These offer picturesque views and tranquil spots for a break.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Segovia?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in the Segovia region are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1107459203/acueducto-alcazar-y-palacio-de-riofrio-circular-desde-segovia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acueducto, Alcázar y Palacio de Riofrío — circular desde Segovia&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1107630495/ermita-de-la-virgen-de-las-vegas-y-navafria-circular-desde-pedraza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de la Virgen de las Vegas y Navafría — circular desde Pedraza&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some longer, more challenging touring cycling routes in the Segovia region?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, Segovia offers several difficult routes, particularly in the Sierra de Guadarrama. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1190502995/la-garganta-del-rio-moros-circular-de-la-estacion-de-el-espinar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Garganta del río Moros — circular de La Estación de El Espinar&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 29-mile (46.7 km) route with significant elevation gain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40018571" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de la Reina – Fuenfría Pass loop from Parque Natural de la Sierra de Guadarrama Norte&lt;/a&gt;, which is over 35 miles (56 km) long and features substantial climbing.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling options in Segovia?
A: Yes, the region provides options suitable for families. The Vías Verdes (Greenways), such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1107493371/eresma-valley-greenway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eresma Valley Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, are often traffic-free routes along old railway lines, making them safer and more enjoyable for families. These routes typically have moderate difficulty and traverse pleasant natural landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Segovia?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Segovia is generally spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is mild, and the landscapes are particularly beautiful. Summers can be hot, especially in the plains, while winters can bring cold temperatures and snow in the higher mountain areas like the Sierra de Guadarrama.

Q: Can I explore medieval villages and castles by bike?
A: Absolutely. The Segovia region is dotted with well-preserved medieval towns like Pedraza, Sepúlveda, and Turegano. Cycling through these areas allows you to explore cobblestone streets, ancient stone walls, and impressive castles, offering a journey back in time. Routes often connect these charming villages, providing cultural stops along your ride.
tips:
The Segovia Aqueduct is an impressive architectural structure that dates back to the 1st century AD. C. and was built by the Romans during the occupation of Hispania. Its main function was to supply water to the city of Segovia, transporting it from the mountains to the urban center. The aqueduct carries the waters of the Fuenfría spring, located in the mountains about 17 kilometers from the city, to the center of Segovia. The water is collected in a cistern called El Caserón and then taken through an ashlar canal to a second tower known as Casa de Aguas. After traveling more than 15 km, you arrive at the city.
The Segovia Aqueduct is a heraldic symbol of the city and one of the most impressive works that the Romans left in their vast empire. If you ever have the opportunity to visit, I recommend that you do so. It's really amazing! 😊🏛️

The Alcázar is shown as the symbol of the old city of Segovia, a World Heritage Site by Unesco.
Its rooms were the resting place of Alfonso X el Sabio (1221-1284), the astronomer king. From them came out, on December 13, 1474, Isabella the Catholic to be proclaimed. The wake mass for the marriage of Felipe II and Ana de Austria (1570) was celebrated in its chapel.

Hunting lodge of the s. XVII-XIX on the land of King Felipe V, who did not see it because it was finished once he died to be offered to his son, Infante Don Luis as a palace in the countryside to retire.
Because Felipe V's wife was Italian, the façade and the interior of the space breathe an Italian air due to the intervention of the architect Virgilio Ravaglio.
For every cyclist who passes by, it is essential to stop at Bosque Riofrío, with a very interesting diversity of fauna, including black and griffon vultures.
The schedule is as follows from May 1 to August 15 from 8:00 a.m. to 9:00 p.m. The rest of the year from 8:00 to sunset.
